reviews.review_description:
I personally love these kinds of games where you solve puzzles, do parkour, and explore.  i did take a 9month break because one section i kept failing at, but i passed it now.
i did fall through the map a lot in this game, but overall it was fun.

reviews.review_title: Love it but way too many bugs
reviews.review_description:
Game is so fun. Graphics, concept and story are engaging; however, the movement-- especially the climbing hinders progress to the point it is often unplayable.
Really hope the developers fix it so I can finish it.

My favorite part of this game is the immersion into the world and all of the different personalities you run into. The bugs are just flat out entertaining to listen to. The puzzles didn't feel so hard that they were anger inducing but not so easy that I just breezed through it all. The scale of a world from the perspective of a bug is something that I didn't realize I wanted but it's just so fantastic to experience. The dev team also cares about their game and the player's experience which is a very big deal to me.  I give it 5 stars because it is absolutely worth the time and money to experience this awesome world and be a bug for a while.

developer_response.body: We're so glad you enjoyed the experience of being a bug!

reviews.review_description:
I played the original game and enjoyed it, so I figured I would try the VR version to see how it was.

In VR, the sense of scale becomes even more impactful. Everyday objects loom like massive structures, and navigating the intricate, miniature world feels both exhilarating and challenging. The tactile design shines in VR, making traversal and puzzle-solving more engaging as you physically look around and interact with the strange environment.

The game’s artistic style and sound design are perfectly suited for VR, drawing you into its bizarre dreamscape. The visuals are intricate and surreal, while the ambient audio and narration make the world feel alive and unsettlingly real

Metamorphosis in VR isn’t just a game; it’s an experience. It’s a must-play for VR enthusiasts who crave innovative storytelling, unique perspectives, and a thought-provoking adventure.
This game is full of fun puzzles, beautiful visuals and fun/unique art.  The only thing I would like to see is a way to "teleport" since walking around with a joystick makes me feel a bit queasy.

reviews.review_title: great game, well worth the price
reviews.review_description: i love how you pick up items directly in your palm and not right near your hand. the movement is quite smooth as well

reviews.review_title: I think I'm turning into a bug
reviews.review_description: Super fun, pace increases shortly into it and unlocks a fun immersion bug sim experience.  Kafka wishes he could do this

reviews.review_title: Game does not work dont waste your money
reviews.review_description: Game sucks and doesnt work

WARNING: Meta store page screenshots and video are PC and super-misleading. Absolutely nothing you see and watch there are even REMOTELY representative of the actual experience quality you get on Quest 3.

VERDICT: Save your money. Get the 100% FREE profile for UEVR if you already have this title on PC. It’s free, and dances absolute circles around this schlock port in terms of polish and graphical fidelity. Believe me, you are NOT missing out on the weirdness that is the motion control scheme. 

Instead of the PC screenshots and trailer they announced the VR version on, you’ll get severely "Potato Moded" graphics, with jarring LOD pop-in's, even at point-blank range. Some objects encountered at close range are just… stuck… on their absolute lowest possible LOD iterations. Zero character/prop shadows, not even just cheesy circular shadow maps under everything. ZERO settings options, so yes you ARE stuck on ‘snap-turning’ like it’s 2018. 

Hope you like clunk controls that have you constantly zinging off to your death, or soaring high above the map after a simple sticky climb, because that’s what you’re working with here. You also get interactive objects that fall apart and/or become little death traps, with various collision/clipping issues abound. 

And on top of all that? We are STILL seeing performance stutters… on a Quest 3 Exclusive! 

At this point, I would have settled for simple 3D skyboxes or a super-compressed G-splat to represent all the distant scenery and high-end lighting outside the walkable areas of each level to save on performance resources... you know… if it actually got me better textures and shadows at least. Lipstick on a pig, given everything else.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you didn't have an enjoyable experience. As I'm sure you can understand, some of the levels, particularly the very large ones, required optimization to run smoothly on the Quest 3. That said, we are working very hard to improve overall visual fidelity, locomotion, collision detection, clipping and popping, and will be rolling out updates over the next several days.

reviews.review_description:
BUGS!!! No seriously, you get to be one :3

This game is absolutely wild! I didn’t think I’d be climbing walls with the mighty power of glue or jumping and scurrying about with my own huge buggy arms but heck this is fun! 

And the narrative behind it all is truly incredible, definitely worth deep diving into :3

Thank you Black Sun Productions for making such a cool game!

I’ve had a fun time playing the game with only a few bugs that didn’t bother with the game experience at all

It feels nice the movement could get worked on but overall a very good game overall

I never thought I'd enjoy being a cockroach so much! 
It really gives me an Alice in wonderland vibe, recommended👍
A few stutters here and there, but it wasn't a big deal
If you get motion sickness this probably isn't the game for you. There's no option for teleport movement unfortunately. It does look like a fun game though.

I decided to try out this new game and o was very impressed. There were no issues and the first part of the game is very simple. While I struggled on one part of the game at the beginning, I eventually found my way through. And the graphics look very detailed and visually pleasing.
I would recommend:)

reviews.review_title: What the curse, I'm a bug!
reviews.review_description:
I'm amazed! My first experience with this game was nothing short of incredible. It starts out simple enough: you're just a guy waking up after a long night out, no big deal. But then things get weird—really weird. And that’s exactly what makes it so great! Very few games offer an experience like this. For me, it was a jaw-dropping, "oh my gosh" moment, where I experienced a new level of scale that was absolutely stunning.

Navigating around as a bug feels exactly like it should. You crawl, hop from place to place, and everything around you is huge. You look up and truly feel the perspective of being a tiny bug. Every detail feels right, and it’s just pure fun.

Then there’s the story—an actual, well-developed story in a VR game! It's wonderfully crafted and full of surprises, and it’s clear the developers poured their hearts into it. They wanted to share their love for this world with you, and it shows.

This game is definitely not worth missing! The perspective is wild, and always what I’ve hoped for and never seen achieved in VR. Humans are gigantic, being on a chair gives the same anxiety as a real drop (and experience, if you’re as terrible as me). The regular updates have been excellent for smoothing out the experience and gameplay. The puzzles are the perfect balance between challenging and fun!

developer_response.body: Great to hear you enjoyed the game!

reviews.review_description: It’s a great game. Super creative and highly interesting

reviews.review_title: VR take on a classic story
reviews.review_description: Seeing Kafka’s work reimagined in VR was unexpected, but I’m glad it was. The story is immersive, with a fantastic sense of scale, and being a bug brings a surprising amount of fun.

developer_response.body: Great to hear you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: GREAT.I really liked the game it was fun and kinda scary since I’m like deathly afraid of insects but it was fun playing as one so overall I rate the game a 4.I just didn’t like that I couldn’t hold two things in my hand though but other than that it was a great game definitely recommend.

Metamorphosis was one of the first VR games I ever played. It’s beginner friendly, easier to learn, and lots of fun! The story is interesting and the VR immersive experience is incredible. You truly feel like an insect in the world. 10/10 would recommend to friends
